authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC compliance guidance for the live-event ticket fees rule covering total-price disclosure, mandatory versus optional charges, misleading fee descriptions, and rule scope.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: price display review, fee disclosure checks, and refundability communication
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains ticketing scenarios so the lane asks whether the displayed price included mandatory charges, avoids mislabeling fees, and refuses unsupported fee waivers or misleading customer messaging.

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Ticket Sales
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DOJ ADA technical guidance for accessible ticket sales, companion seating, hold and release rules, secondary-market exchanges, and comparable sales-channel requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible seating intake, hold and release handling, and transfer or exchange boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from improvising accessible inventory handling, steering accessible buyers into separate channels, or weakening comparable-access rules when event pressure is high.

authoritative_sources.title: How can I get a refund on a product or service I purchased with my credit card?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB guidance defining when a purchase dispute becomes a card-issuer billing-error matter, which is authoritative for merchant-versus-issuer boundaries when ticket refunds, non-delivery, or event changes are disputed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch review, refund status handling, and issuer-boundary escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from overstating merchant authority when the issue has crossed into card-dispute handling and forces packaging of the right ticketing and payment evidence for the next owner.

authoritative_sources.title: BOTS Act compliance: Time for a refresher?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC business guidance describing BOTS Act enforcement posture, prohibited circumvention of posted event ticket limits, and liability for tickets obtained through illegal automated purchasing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ticket-limit enforcement, suspicious order review, and anti-abuse escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane from approving manual exceptions that would bypass ticket limits, transfer controls, or anti-abuse measures just because the guest impact feels urgent.

authoritative_sources.title: "That's the Ticket" Workshop: Staff Perspective
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC staff report summarizing public workshop findings on transferability, bots, all-in pricing, price obfuscation, consumer injury, and ticket-market workflow terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ticket-market terminology grounding, transferability handling, and queue design assumptions
used_for: all-in pricing and fee-obfuscation failure modes
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Provides ticketing-specific terminology and market-shape context so the overlay stays specific to holds, transfers, resale, price obfuscation, and anti-bot workflows instead of generic guest-service language.

authoritative_sources.title: Venue Guide for Security Considerations
authoritative_sources.publisher: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, U.S. Department of Homeland Security
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CISA venue-operator guidance covering access control, crowd management, entry requirements, emergency communications, electronic ticket scanning, and escalation to public-safety partners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event-day access control, screening, crowd management, and emergency escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Stops the lane from improvising security or safety outcomes when scan failures, blocked accessible routes, or entry-screening changes require venue-operations or public-safety ownership.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards and Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Event Safety Alliance is a recognized event-industry framework publisher, and its public Event Safety Guide is a vendor-neutral operational reference for crowd management, communication, emergency planning, and venue safety coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event workflow readiness, patron communication, and cross-team safety coordination
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds a recognized event-operations framework so the lane can summarize ticketing readiness and handoffs without pretending to own security command or emergency decision-making.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and event-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm event, venue, date, order or booking identifiers, channel, ticket class, accessibility context, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane before responding.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Order, seat, payment, and access record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate order status, seat or section assignment, price and fee presentation, promotion impacts, refund or exchange state, barcode or credential status, scan history, and prior service notes so the answer stays tied to authoritative records.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported ticketing resolution and bounded communication
workflow_stages.description: Provide evidence-backed ticketing status, delegated next steps, and customer or stakeholder communication without promising refunds, seating changes, admission overrides, or safety outcomes that require another owner.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Event-day queue triage and workflow packaging
workflow_stages.description: Sort high-volume queues by request class, event timing, access impact, and authority boundary while preserving ticketing-specific terminology and readiness blockers.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Policy exception, fraud, and safety escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move unsupported refund or pricing exceptions, suspicious ticket activity, accessible-seating conflicts, blocked entry routes, and safety-sensitive access issues into explicit escalation packets with the correct owner and evidence set.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: orders, bookings, reservations, customer and guest profiles, and communication history
seat maps, seat holds, presale allocations, accessible inventory records, companion-seat records, and hold-release logs
barcode, credential, transfer, reissue, will-call, manifest, and scan-history records
payment tender history, fee displays, refund and exchange records, chargeback markers, and promotion or comp records
event schedules, access lists, venue-entry policies, incident notes, and event-readiness summaries
fraud or loss-prevention notes, duplicate-ticket flags, security or safety routing notes, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: event management or venue operations system

allowed: Classify ticketing requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, exception-driven, event-readiness-sensitive, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed status, delegated ticketing handling, next-step summaries, and customer communication without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packages and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required order, seat, scan, refund, accessibility, promoter-term, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, discretionary comp approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, or venue-security signoff.
An accessibility, blocked-entry, suspicious-ticket, duplicate-barcode, or safety signal changes whether ticketing can act directly.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, promoter, platform, processor, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Promise a refund, fee waiver, seat upgrade, ticket transfer, or entry override that the authoritative record and policy set do not support.
Override pricing, refund, accessibility, anti-bot, admission, or safety controls without explicit authority.
Authorize venue entry or claim a route or condition is safe when the case is safety-sensitive.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises a refund, fee waiver, or seat exception when the order record, tenant policy, or approval map does not support it
a booking, order, or payment mismatch is handled as ordinary customer service instead of being routed into the correct refund, access-control, or processor path
a ticketing case closes without the minimum event, venue, order, seat, or payment evidence
accessible seating is treated as a courtesy issue instead of being governed by DOJ ticket-sales requirements
duplicate-ticket or suspicious transfer signals are ignored in the name of event-day convenience
ticketing staff give unsafe reassurance on blocked entry routes, crowd-flow restrictions, or security-screening conflicts
the industry overlay collapses into generic support language instead of ticketing terminology, artifacts, and event-delivery control boundaries

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State or local event-cancellation and refund rules
unresolved_ambiguities.details: Refund timing and mandatory-remedy rules may vary by jurisdiction, venue contract, processor rules, and event type. The pack therefore requires tenant jurisdiction and policy retrieval before final refund handling.
Public safety guidance establishes the escalation shape, but actual screening, prohibited-item, and emergency-action rules remain venue-specific and must be retrieved at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Secondary-market transfer settings and resale permissions
unresolved_ambiguities.details: Public sources clarify accessible-seat and anti-bot boundaries, but transferability and resale allowances can vary by state law, promoter terms, and platform configuration.

notes: Research coverage satisfies the retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract by covering consumer protection, booking and refund operations, and safety or loss-prevention handling.
No unresolved ambiguity is blocking for a validated spec pack because each open point is explicitly converted into a runtime retrieval dependency or escalation rule.
